name: Remote Code Execution via Unauthenticated Scenario Restore OS Command Injection
description: The Flask Dashboard application at http://dashboard.example-domain.com contains a critical OS command injection vulnerability through the scenario restore mechanism. An unauthenticated attacker can create a scenario with an arbitrary shell command in the `restore.command` field via POST /api/arch/save-scenario, start a run via POST /api/scenario-run/<scenario_id>, and then trigger execution of that command via POST /api/scenario-run/<run_id>/restore. The user-controlled restore.command value is passed directly to subprocess.run(['bash', '-c', cmd]) without any authentication, authorization, or input sanitization. The command executes as root (uid=0) in a bash shell context, and the output is returned directly in the HTTP response. This constitutes OS-level command injection rather than application-language code injection, as the attacker-supplied input is interpreted by the bash shell (not the Python interpreter), enabling arbitrary system command execution.
expected_root_cause: COMMAND_INJECTION 취약점 — Remote Code Execution via Unauthenticated Scenario Restore OS Command Injection
